taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about skin and fascia.
textField:
The two main types of skin are *thick* skin (found on palms and soles) and *thin* skin (covering the rest of the body).

A *dermatome* is the area of skin supplied by a single spinal nerve.

The skin weighs about *3.5* kg and covers approximately *2* square metres.

Surgical incisions made along *Langer's* lines heal with minimal scarring because they follow the orientation of collagen fibres in the dermis.

The superficial fascia (also called *subcutaneous* tissue) contains fat, cutaneous nerves, and superficial blood vessels.

The two layers of the dermis are the superficial *papillary* layer and the deeper *reticular* layer.

The epidermis is composed of *keratinised* stratified squamous epithelium and contains *melanocytes* that produce pigment.

Deep fascia forms *retinacula* at the wrist and ankle that hold tendons in place.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review skin and fascia features and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent understanding of skin and fascia!
